search_metadata.query: how to build a distributed database

results.title: Building a Distributed Database from Scratch: A Complete Guide
results.description: A comprehensive tutorial covering the fundamentals of distributed database design, including consensus protocols, data partitioning strategies, replication, and consistency models. Updated for 2025 with examples using Raft and multi-Paxos.

results.title: CockroachDB Architecture: How a Distributed SQL Database Works
results.description: Deep dive into CockroachDB's distributed architecture covering the SQL layer, transaction layer, distribution layer, replication layer, and storage layer. Learn how a production-grade distributed database handles automatic sharding and failover.

results.title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications - Chapter 5: Replication
results.description: Martin Kleppmann's authoritative guide on replication in distributed systems. Covers leader-based replication, multi-leader replication, leaderless replication, and the tradeoffs between synchronous and asynchronous approaches.

results.title: How TiDB Implements a Distributed Database with MySQL Compatibility
results.description: Technical blog post explaining TiDB's architecture including TiKV (distributed key-value storage), PD (placement driver), and TiDB server (SQL parsing). Includes performance benchmarks comparing single-node MySQL to distributed TiDB clusters.

results.title: CS 244B: Distributed Systems - Stanford University (Lecture Notes)
results.description: Lecture notes from Stanford's graduate course on distributed systems covering CAP theorem, ACID vs BASE, vector clocks, Lamport timestamps, two-phase commit, three-phase commit, and the FLP impossibility result.

results.title: YouTube: Building a Distributed Key-Value Store in Go (Full Tutorial)
results.description: A 3-hour video tutorial walking through building a distributed key-value store from scratch in Go. Covers implementing the Raft consensus algorithm, building a WAL (write-ahead log), and setting up cluster membership changes.

results.title: Amazon DynamoDB: How It Works Under the Hood
results.description: AWS engineers explain the internal architecture of DynamoDB including consistent hashing for partition management, Paxos-based replication for strong consistency, and the storage node architecture. Includes the original Dynamo paper concepts.

results.title: Jepsen: Testing Distributed Databases for Correctness
results.description: Kyle Kingsbury's comprehensive testing framework and analysis reports for distributed databases. Covers linearizability testing, network partition simulation, and correctness verification for systems including CockroachDB, YugabyteDB, TiDB, and FoundationDB.

results.title: Google Spanner: Globally-Distributed Database - Research Paper
results.description: The original Google Spanner paper describing how Google built a globally-distributed, synchronously-replicated database. Key innovations include TrueTime API using atomic clocks and GPS receivers to provide globally consistent timestamps.

results.title: FoundationDB: A Practical Guide to Building on a Distributed Database
results.description: Apple's guide to building applications on FoundationDB, covering the layer concept, ordered key-value store semantics, multi-key ACID transactions, and how to design custom data models on top of FoundationDB's minimal but powerful primitives.

related_searches: cockroachdb vs tidb vs yugabytedb comparison

knowledge_panel.title: Distributed Database
knowledge_panel.description: A distributed database is a database in which data is stored across multiple physical locations. It may be spread across multiple computers located in the same physical location or dispersed over a network of interconnected computers. Unlike parallel systems, distributed databases typically operate over a wide area network and allow geographic distribution of data.

key_facts.label: Notable examples
key_facts.value: Google Spanner, CockroachDB, TiDB, YugabyteDB, FoundationDB
